sections.paragraph:
com. (compare Gen 32:9 Psa 27:3), from the root חָנָה.

(1) a camp, whether of soldiers, Jos 6:11 1Sa 14:15, seq.; or of wandering tribes, Exo 16:13 Num 4:5, 15 5:2 10:34 11:1 , Num 11:9, 30 Num 11:30, 31 Num 11:31. Hence-

(2) an army itself, Exo 14:24 Jud. 4:16 a band of men, Gen 50:9 of locusts [?] Joe 2:11 also a herd of cattle, Gen 33:8.

The plural is formed in three different ways-(a) מַחֲנִים encampments, Num 13:19 with suff. מַחֲנֶיךָ, מַחֲנֵיחֶם Deu 23:15 Jos 10:5 11:4 .-(b) מַחֲנֹות Gen 32:8, 11 Gen 32:11 Num 10:2, Num 10:5, Num 10:6. מַחֲנֹות יְהֹוָה “the courts of Jehovah,” in which the priests as it were encamped, 2Ch 31:2.-(c) מַחֲנַיִם from sing. מַחֲנַי (compare מְמֻחַיִם under the word מָחָה No. III, and Lehrg. p. 537), Son 7:1, there used of the heavenly host (צְבָאֹות), or angels (comp. Gen 32:3), to which the poet [say rather, inspired writer] ascribes dances, as elsewhere songs ( Job 38:7). Comp. pr.n. מַחֲנַיִם, which some also understand frigidly enough in the passage in Cant.
